REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501,PLAINTIFF CATHAY BANK, A CALIFORNIA BANKING CORPORATION MOTION / NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR AN ORDER APPOINTING A RENTS. ISSUES AND PROFITS RECEIVER FOR THE REAL PROPERTY COMMONLY KNOWN AS 25 AND 33 DRUMM STREET, SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA, AND A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IN AID OF THE RECEIVER; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS & AUTHORITIES IS CONTINUED TO JUNE 28, 2024. NO LATER THAN JUNE 7, 2024 THE MOVING PARTY TO FILE A LIST SPECIFYING THE DOCUMENT TO BE PRODUCED BY THE DEFENDANT GENERALLY REFERENCED IN THE REPLY AS "ALL REQUIRED BOOKS, RECORDS, INCOME AND EXPENSE STATEMENTS, AND FINANCIALS FOR THE PROPERTY." NO LATER THAN JUNE 20, 2024 DEFENDANT TO PRODUCED SUCH DOCUMENTS TO THE PLAINTIFF AND FILE A DECLARATION WITH THE COURT NO LATER THAN 5:00 P.M. ON JUNE 20, 2024 ATTESTING TO SUCH PRODUCTION. NO LATER THAN JUNE 26, 2024 PLAINTIFF TO FILE A REPLY EITHER CONFIRMING THE RECEIPT OF ALL REQUESTED DOCUMENTS OR SPECIFYING THE DOCUMENTS THAT HAVE NOT BEEN PRODUCED. ADDITIONALLY, NO LATER THAN JUNE 20, 2024 PLAINTIFF TO FILE A SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF (NOT TO EXCEED 2 PAGES) AND DECLARATION(S) WITH PLAINTIFF'S/PROPOSED RECEIVER'S SUGGESTIONS AS TO THE HIRING OF A DIFFERENT LISTING BROKER WHO IS READY AND WILLING TO MARKET THE PROPERTY FOR RENT. SUCH BROKER(S) SHALL BE IDENTIFIED AND THE ESTIMATED TIME FOR LOCATING NEW TENANTS SHALL BE PROVIDED. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: VANESSA WU; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR INSTRUCTIONS RE TURNOVER OF RENTS IS GRANTED. JOHN YEE AND LILY YEE ARE ORDERED TO SHOW CAUSE ON JULY 16, 2026 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPT. 501 WHY THEY SHOULD NOT HAVE TO IMMEDIATELY TURN OVER ALL "CASH PROCEEDS" DERIVED FROM THE SUBJECT PROPERTY, INCLUDING RENTAL INCOME, THAT WAS COLLECTED BY THE YEES. PARTIES APPEARING IN THIS HEARING STIPULATE TO THE ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE HEARING DATE OF JULY 16, 2026 AT 9:30 A.M. NOTICE IS WAIVED.VISITING JUDGE: DENNIS HAYASHI; CLERK: K. VALDES; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

OTHER BELLANN RAILE RECEIVER Notice Of Motion And Motion For 1.Approval Of Prior Receivers Finalaccount And Report; 2.Approval Of Actions Of Prior Receiver; 3.Approval Of Fees And Expenses Ofprior Receiver And Her Professionals; 4.Approval Of Anticipated Fees And Expenses Of Prior Receiver And Her Professionals; 5.Barring Future Claims Againstprior Receiver And Her Professionals; And 6.Exoneration Of Prior Receiver Sbonds

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Instructions Re Turnover Of Rents
Off Calendar May-29-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances
Mark Romeo, Esq. for Defendant Epic Properties.
Bruce Poltrock, Esq. for Plaintiff Cathay Bank via Zoom Video Webinar.
Kenneth Hsu, Esq. for receiver Blake Alsbrook who is also present via Zoom Video Webinar.

Hearing held.

Matter is argued.
After hearing the arguments, the Court rules as follows:

OTHER BLAKE ALSBROOK (RECEIVER) Notice Of Motion And Motion For Instructions Re Turnover Of Rents

is GRANTED. John Yee and Lily Yee are ordered to show cause on July 16, 2026 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 501 why they should not have to immediately turn over all "cash proceeds" derived from the Subject Property, including rental income, that was collected by the Yees.

Parties appearing in this hearing stipulate to the order to show cause hearing date of July 16, 2026 at 9:30 a.m. Notice is waived.

Visiting Judge: Dennis Hayashi; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)

MOTION / Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Appointing A Rents. Issues And Profits Receiver For The Real Property Commonly Known As 25 And 33 Drumm Street, San Francisco, California, And A Preliminary Injunction In Aid Of The Receiver; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities
Off Calendar Jun-28-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Bruce Poltrock; 323-852-1000; Frandzel Robins Bloom & Csato, L.C.; appearing via Zoom for plaintiff.
Mark Joseph Romeo; 415-395-9315; Law Offices of Mark J. Romeo, PC; appearing for defendant.
David Blatteis-listing broker from Blatteis Realty.

Hearing held regarding various supplemental issues. Not argued. The Court rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF CATHAY BANK, A CALIFORNIA BANKING CORPORATION MOTION / Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Appointing A Rents. Issues And Profits Receiver For The Real Property Commonly Known As 25 And 33 Drumm Street, San Francisco, California, And A Preliminary Injunction In Aid Of The Receiver; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities is GRANTED.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)

Judge: Charles F. Haines ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)

MOTION / Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Appointing A Rents. Issues And Profits Receiver For The Real Property Commonly Known As 25 And 33 Drumm Street, San Francisco, California, And A Preliminary Injunction In Aid Of The Receiver; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities
Off Calendar May-23-2024 Continued To Jun-28-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

PLAINTIFF CATHAY BANK, A CALIFORNIA BANKING CORPORATION MOTION / Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Appointing A Rents. Issues And Profits Receiver For The Real Property Commonly Known As 25 And 33 Drumm Street, San Francisco, California, And A Preliminary Injunction In Aid Of The Receiver; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities is continued to June 28, 2024. No later than June 7, 2024 the moving party to file a list specifying the document to be produced by the Defendant generally referenced in the reply as "all required books, records, income and expense statements, and financials for the Property." No later than June 20, 2024 Defendant to produced such documents to the Plaintiff and file a declaration with the Court no later than 5:00 p.m. on June 20, 2024 attesting to such production. No later than June 26, 2024 Plaintiff to file a reply either confirming the receipt of all requested documents or specifying the documents that have not been produced.
Additionally, no later than June 20, 2024 Plaintiff to file a supplemental brief (not to exceed 2 pages) and declaration(s) with Plaintiff's/proposed receiver's suggestions as to the hiring of a different listing broker who is ready and willing to market the property for rent. Such broker(s) shall be identified and the estimated time for locating new tenants shall be provided.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)
